new_value: Extension labels now rendered as prominent text on code and default document icons in tiles view. Font size adapts to label length.
Added subtle radial gradient vignette to content area via --content-bg CSS variable. Custom tints for dark, hacker, and ocean-blue themes.
all steps complete
Set caret-color to accent color for clear visibility in the path input.
Added open_image_with_siblings command that detects the default image viewer and passes all sibling images, enabling arrow-key navigation in imv, feh, sxiv, etc.
Fixed: same-directory cut-paste no longer falsely detects conflicts or tries to move a file onto itself. The operation skips the no-op move and completes immediately.
Fixed: conflict dialog Cancel now properly marks the operation as cancelled in the operations manager, allowing the progress dialog to show the correct cancelled state.
Drag-and-drop now checks for filename conflicts in the target directory and shows the conflict resolution modal (replace/skip/cancel) before proceeding. Applied to all view modes.
Backend optimized: entry.file_type() avoids stat syscalls, first-batch 16ms, steady 100ms. Frontend pagination was already in place (PAGE_SIZE=200, virtual scroll, incremental flatten).
Frontend pagination already implemented: PAGE_SIZE=200, infinite scroll on scroll-to-bottom, virtual scrolling with binary search. No changes needed.
all steps complete
Preview pane is already implemented and functional: supports image thumbnails, text file preview, PDF preview, and file metadata display. Component: PreviewPane.svelte.
Added .theme-background-layer div with z-index:-1, accepting --theme-background-image and --theme-background-color CSS variables. Respects --bg-opacity.
Added --sidebar-opacity, --toolbar-opacity, --content-opacity, --titlebar-opacity, --statusbar-opacity CSS tokens. Each section uses color-mix to apply its token. Defaults to 1 in light/dark themes.

{"id":"tauri-jsn1.4","title":"Implement swappable icon theme system","description":"Extend icon rendering beyond the current color-tint approach to support full icon theme packs. Design:
- Define an IconTheme interface: { id, name, folderIcon, folderOpenIcon, fileIcon, fileExtensionMap }
- Each icon theme provides SVG components or SVG strings for folder/file icons
- Themes can declare --theme-icon-pack: 'default' | 'material' | 'minimal' etc.
- File extension map allows per-extension custom icons (e.g., .ts, .rs, .md)
- Default icon theme preserves current behavior
- Support external icon packs, particularly material theme icons (same as yazi uses)
  - Yazi uses https://github.com/catppuccin/yazi and material-themed icon packs
  - We should support a similar icon pack format: mapping file extensions/names to Nerd Font glyphs or SVG icons
  - Reference yazi icon pack structure for compatibility/familiarity
- Ship with built-in themes: 'default' (current), 'material' (Material Design file icons), 'rounded' (softer shapes)
- Icon themes are independent of color themes but can be paired via theme CSS
- Allow users to install/load custom icon packs from a config directory","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"claudeuser@example.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T10:24:18Z","created_by":"Claude User","updated_at":"2026-03-04T10:38:54Z"}

new_value: Implemented swappable icon theme system: added 'minimal' built-in theme (monochrome outlines), --theme-icon-pack CSS variable for themes to suggest preferred icon packs, effectiveIconTheme getter that respects theme CSS var when user is on 'default'. Hacker theme auto-selects minimal icons. Settings UI updated with all three options.
{"status":"in_progress"}

{"id":"tauri-jsn1.5","title":"Extend breadcrumb and chrome theming tokens","description":"Add more CSS custom properties for navigation bar and window chrome styling so themes can achieve distinctive looks (like Aurora's colored breadcrumb segments). New tokens:
- --breadcrumb-bg: background color/gradient for breadcrumb segments
- --breadcrumb-text: text color inside breadcrumb segments
- --breadcrumb-active-bg: background for the current/last segment
- --breadcrumb-hover-bg: hover state background
- --breadcrumb-radius: border-radius for segments
- --breadcrumb-gap: spacing between segments
- --toolbar-border-bottom: bottom border style for toolbar area
- --sidebar-border-right: right border style for sidebar
Existing themes should get sensible defaults. The Hacker theme already has breadcrumb overrides (powerline separators) — ensure those remain working.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"claudeuser@example.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T10:24:28Z","created_by":"Claude User","updated_at":"2026-03-04T10:24:28Z"}

new_value: Added breadcrumb theming tokens (--breadcrumb-bg, --breadcrumb-text, --breadcrumb-active-bg, --breadcrumb-hover-bg, --breadcrumb-radius, --breadcrumb-gap) and chrome tokens (--toolbar-border-bottom, --sidebar-border-right, --navbar-border-bottom). All use fallback values preserving existing behavior.

Closing as deferred — blocked by upstream wry #1524 (WebKitGTK ghosting bug). Investigation summary already in notes: removing WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1 enables transparency but causes progressive opacity accumulation. Compositor-side opacity (hyprctl) doesn't persist across focus changes. Will revisit when wry #1524 is resolved upstream.

old_value:
{"id":"tauri-jsn1.3","title":"Implement animated background renderer system","description":"Build a pluggable animated background system that themes can use to render canvas-based effects (particle fields, starfields, matrix rain, etc.). Design:
- Theme CSS declares --theme-bg-animation: 'particles' | 'starfield' | 'none' (default)
- A registry maps animation names to render functions: (canvas: HTMLCanvasElement, colors: ThemeColors) => cleanup()
- The background layer (from parent issue) hosts an optional <canvas> element
- Renderer is activated/deactivated on theme switch
- Respects prefers-reduced-motion media query (disables animations)
- Uses requestAnimationFrame with automatic pause when window is not visible
- Ship with at least two built-in animations: 'particles' (floating dots with connections) and 'starfield' (twinkling stars with constellation lines, like Aurora Explorer)
Depends on the background layer issue being completed first.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"claudeuser@example.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T10:23:47Z","created_by":"Claude User","updated_at":"2026-03-04T10:23:47Z"}

new_value: Implemented pluggable animated background system. Registry maps animation names to render functions. Two built-in animations: particles (floating dots with connections) and starfield (twinkling stars with constellations). Themes opt in via --theme-bg-animation CSS var. Respects prefers-reduced-motion. Auto-pauses when window hidden. Aurora uses starfield, Hacker uses particles.
{"status":"in_progress"}

{"id":"tauri-jsn1.6","title":"Add user-selectable wallpaper/background image setting","description":"Allow users to pick a custom background image from their filesystem, independent of theme. This complements the theme-provided backgrounds with user personalization. Design:
- Add a 'Background Image' option in settings (file picker dialog via Tauri)
- Supported formats: PNG, JPG, WEBP, SVG
- Image is rendered in the background layer with cover sizing and center positioning
- User image takes precedence over theme-declared --theme-background-image
- Add a 'Clear' button to remove the custom wallpaper and revert to theme default
- Store the path in settingsStore (persisted to localStorage)
- Add a --user-bg-blur token (0-20px) so users can blur their wallpaper for readability
Depends on the background layer issue.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"claudeuser@example.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T10:24:38Z","created_by":"Claude User","updated_at":"2026-03-04T10:24:38Z"}

new_value: Added user-selectable wallpaper with path input, clear button, and blur slider (0-20px). Wallpaper renders via Tauri asset protocol in the background layer. User image takes precedence over theme-declared backgrounds. Asset protocol scope enabled in tauri.conf.json.

old_value:
{"id":"tauri-jsn1.8","title":"Enable true window transparency on Linux (see-through to desktop)","description":"Remove WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1 env var from src-tauri/src/lib.rs which blocks WebKitGTK alpha channel rendering. With this removed, the existing transparent:true config + CSS color-mix opacity will actually show the desktop/wallpaper behind the window when background opacity is lowered. Note: backdrop-filter:blur() only blurs within-DOM content, not the desktop. For desktop blur, users configure their compositor (e.g. Hyprland windowrule blur). Test that removing the env var doesn't reintroduce the Wayland protocol errors it was originally added to fix.","notes":"   1 ## Investigation Summary (2026-03-04)
   2 
   3 ### What was tried
   4 
   5 1. **Removed WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1** — This allowed WebKitGTK alpha channel rendering. Window transparency DID work (wallpaper visible), but suffered from a known ghosting bug (wry #1524): every interaction causes stale frames to accumulate, making the window progressively more opaque.
   6 
   7 2. **Disabled backdrop-filter when opacity < 100%** — WebKitGTK creates an opaque compositing surface for elements with backdrop-filter, blocking transparent pixels from reaching the compositor. Conditionally disabling it didn't fully resolve the ghosting.
   8 
   9 3. **Forced GDK_BACKEND=wayland** — To avoid XWayland breaking GTK RGBA transparency.
  10 
  11 4. **Compositor-side opacity via hyprctl** — Restored WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1 (no ghosting) and used \`hyprctl dispatch setprop pid:SELF opacity VALUE lock\` to delegate transparency to Hyprland. This worked when the window was focused, but opacity reverted on focus loss. Tried space-separated \`opacity "active inactive"\` syntax — still didn't persist.
  12 
  13 ### Key findings
  14 
  15 - **WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1** blocks ALL window transparency but prevents ghosting artifacts
  16 - **CSS backdrop-filter: blur()** only blurs within-DOM content, NOT the desktop behind the window
  17 - **Tauri windowEffects / window-vibrancy** don't support Linux at all
  18 - **hyprctl setprop opacity** has unreliable persistence across focus changes on Hyprland 0.53.3
  19 - The ghosting bug is tracked in wry #1524 (open, labeled upstream)
  20 - User's WebKitGTK version: 2.50.5, Hyprland: 0.53.3
  21 
  22 ### Possible future approaches
  23 
  24 1. Wait for wry #1524 / upstream WebKitGTK fix for the ghosting bug
  25 2. Investigate Hyprland windowrule (in hyprland.conf, not runtime setprop) as a more persistent mechanism
  26 3. Look into custom Wayland protocol for blur-behind (KDE blur protocol, wlr-foreign-toplevel)
  27 4. Try forcing redraws via window resize hack on every interaction (janky but might work)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"claudeuser@example.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T10:36:31Z","created_by":"Claude User","updated_at":"2026-03-05T07:30:48Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T07:30:48Z","close_reason":"Closing as deferred — blocked by upstream wry #1524 (WebKitGTK ghosting bug). Investigation summary already in notes: removing WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1 enables transparency but causes progressive opacity accumulation. Compositor-side opacity (hyprctl) doesn't persist across focus changes. Will revisit when wry #1524 is resolved upstream."}
